purpose: Curated lightweight resource database for Nora education suggestions. Store links and short metadata only; do not store full article text.

storage_decision.reason: JSON is easy for Nora/tools to filter by domain, topic, format, trigger keywords, and user content preference. Full article HTML is intentionally not stored.

rejected_alternate.title: Dollar Cost Averaging (DCA): Complete Beginner's Guide
rejected_alternate.reason: Good beginner title, but the page is older, has a stronger trading-course/promotional feel, and includes more assertive payoff language. The selected article is newer and has clearer sections for DCA vs lump sum and market timing.

dollar_cost_averaging_choice.nora_usage_note: When recommending the selected DCA article, Nora should frame it as consistency and timing-risk reduction, not as a promise of better returns or profit.

resources.title: An essential guide to building an emergency fund
resources.summary: A practical guide to what an emergency fund is, why it matters, how to build one, where to keep it, and when to use it.
resources.why_nora_recommends_it: Best after Nora explains why short-term money should stay savings-first before investing.

resources.title: Setting Goals & Time Horizons
resources.summary: A video candidate for explaining how goal timing affects whether money should stay in savings or can be invested.
resources.why_nora_recommends_it: Potentially useful when Nora talks about short-, medium-, and long-term goals, but the downloaded transcript currently contains only the credit line.

resources.title: 5 Strategies to Navigate Market Volatility
resources.summary: Explains that market volatility is a normal part of investing and gives practical strategies for handling it without panic.
resources.why_nora_recommends_it: Useful after the user says risk or market drops make investing feel scary.

resources.title: Stock Market Volatility Explained: How to Navigate & Stay Calm
resources.creator: All Day Live
resources.local_transcript_path: C:\Users\karip\Downloads\Transcripts\2\Stock Market Volatility Explained- How to Navigate & Stay Calm.txt
resources.summary: A conversational video about recent stock market swings and how to think about them without overreacting.
resources.why_nora_recommends_it: Good for users who prefer listening and need risk to feel less dramatic.

resources.title: Start monthly saving
resources.topic: Automatic monthly saving and monthly fund investing
resources.summary: Explains regular monthly saving, automatic transfers, building a buffer first, and moving toward funds when the buffer is in place.
resources.why_nora_recommends_it: The best bank-native resource after Nora drafts a recurring savings habit or explains monthly saving.

resources.title: Your Complete Guide to Dollar Cost Averaging
resources.author: Rebecca Hotsko
resources.summary: Explains dollar-cost averaging, how regular investments work, and the tradeoff between averaging in and investing a lump sum.
resources.why_nora_recommends_it: Good after Nora explains that a small recurring investment can reduce timing pressure.
resources.usage_warning: Avoid repeating any profit-heavy framing. Nora should emphasize consistency and uncertainty, not guaranteed results.

resources.title: The psychology of saving: How to stay motivated on your financial journey
resources.summary: Covers money mindset, emotional triggers, smaller milestones, automation, visual progress, and making future rewards feel tangible.
resources.why_nora_recommends_it: Useful when a goal is safe but motivation-risk is high and Nora suggests a first milestone.

resources.title: Key Financial Milestones to Reach in Your 20s & 30s
resources.summary: A life-stage milestone resource for users who want a broader sense of what to build toward in their 20s and 30s.
resources.why_nora_recommends_it: Useful when Nora wants to connect a single savings goal to a bigger life-stage money path.
missing_data: page content could not be directly fetched

resources.title: How to Invest in Index Funds: A Beginner's Guide to Diversified Investing
resources.author: Wajahat Raja
resources.summary: Explains what index funds are, why diversification and low costs matter, and what beginners should consider before choosing funds.
resources.why_nora_recommends_it: Good when the user is ready for the default beginner investment path after savings-first logic.

resources.title: Stock Picking vs Index Funds - Investing For Beginners
resources.local_transcript_path: C:\Users\karip\Downloads\Transcripts\5\Stock Picking vs Index Funds - Investing For Beginners.txt
resources.summary: Compares stock picking with index funds and frames index funds as a simpler beginner path for many people.
resources.why_nora_recommends_it: Useful if the user asks why Nora starts with funds instead of individual stocks.

resources.title: Interest rates, margins, repayment methods
resources.summary: Explains fixed interest, reference rates, margins, actual annual interest, and repayment concepts from a Finnish consumer-protection perspective.
resources.why_nora_recommends_it: Best when the user asks what loan interest actually means or how repayment affects future monthly freedom.

resources.title: Loan and Behold: Student Loans Explained - Countdown2College Podcast S2 EP36
resources.creator: EducationQuest Foundation / Countdown to College
resources.local_transcript_path: C:\Users\karip\Downloads\Transcripts\6\Loan and Behold- Student Loans Explained - Countdown2College Podcast S2 EP36.txt
resources.summary: Podcast-style beginner discussion about student loans, financial aid, and borrowing for college.
resources.why_nora_recommends_it: Good for users who prefer listening and mention student loans or education borrowing.

missing_data_summary.impact: Usable as a link candidate, but not good for transcript-based scanning until the transcript is redownloaded.
